subject: Ecology
title: Self-Pollination vs Cross-Pollination
description: A 1-minute de-risking session on Self-Pollination vs Cross-Pollination.

hook.text: Some flowers pollinate themselves. Others insist on pollen from a completely different plant.

buildup.text: Self-pollination guarantees reproduction but produces genetically identical offspring.

discovery.text: Cross-pollination mixes genes, creating diversity that helps species adapt to change.

twist.text: Many plants have mechanisms to prevent self-pollination—timing, chemistry, or physical barriers.

climax.text: Some species can self-pollinate as a backup when no pollinators visit—a survival insurance policy.

punchline.text: Mixing genes is worth the extra effort.

quiz.question: Why is cross-pollination advantageous?
options: It increases genetic diversity for adaptation
